ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general toast bread product class; no specific entry for kaya toast with butter exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers toasted bread generically; no specific kaya toast composite entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for kaya toast as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of kaya toast consists of 2 slices of thin white bread (approximately 80 g total), each toasted and spread with kaya coconut jam and a thin pat of cold butter.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya Toast — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA SR Legacy composite estimate for white toast bread with kaya and butter
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived by combining HPB Singapore Foodo data for kaya toast (item: Kaya Toast, 2 slices) with USDA SR Legacy values for white bread (USDA FDC ID 269714) and butter (USDA FDC ID 173410) and kaya jam (coconut egg jam). Per-100g values represent the composite edible portion of 2 slices of white bread (~40g bread) with kaya (~15g) and butter (~8g) spread, yielding approximately 80g total serving. Values normalised to per-100g basis. HPB Foodo reports approximately 280 kcal per 2-slice serving of kaya butter toast; USDA SR Legacy used for micronutrient detail.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — white bread GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore kaya toast as a composite dish. GI of 70 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for white bread (GI ~70, glucose reference), which is the dominant glycemic component. Kaya jam adds sugar (raising effective GI slightly) while butter moderates glycemic response marginally. Net GI estimated at 70 (high). Glycemic load per 80g serving = (70 × 38.4g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 26.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100g) and butter contribution
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit kaya toast to one serving occasionally, as the sodium from bread and butter (~256 mg per 80g serving) contributes meaningfully toward the 2000 mg daily sodium target recommended by HPB Singapore.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 70) and significant sugar load from kaya jam
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kaya toast carries a high glycemic index (~70) driven by refined white bread and sugar-rich kaya jam, producing a glycemic load of approximately 27 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this food sparingly, preferably substituting wholemeal bread and reducing kaya quantity to lower postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter (~7.5g/100g) and refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat contributed by butter (~6g per 80g serving) and the refined carbohydrate load from white bread and kaya are relevant for cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and consider wholemeal bread alternatives with reduced butter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kaya toast is a quintessential Singaporean breakfast and snack dish served at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) and cafes across Singapore. It consists of two thin slices of white bread that are toasted until golden and crisp, then spread generously with kaya — a sweet, fragrant coconut and egg jam flavoured with pandan leaves — and a thin slab of cold salted butter. The contrast between the warm, crunchy toast, the sweet aromatic kaya, and the cold creamy butter is central to its appeal. Kaya toast is traditionally served alongside soft-boiled eggs seasoned with soy sauce and white pepper, and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea). The dish has Hainanese origins, introduced by early Chinese immigrants who adapted Western toast to local flavour preferences. Nutritionally, kaya toast is a moderate-energy food (~280 kcal per 2-slice serving) with significant carbohydrate content from white bread and sugar from kaya jam, making it a high-GI food. The butter contributes saturated fat. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease without modification. Variants include peanut butter toast, kaya-only toast, and versions using wholemeal or thick-cut bread.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kaya toast?
What is kaya jam made of?
Is kaya toast high in sugar?

confidence_reason: No single HPB Foodo entry precisely matches the composite kaya-butter-peanut toast as served by The Light Cafe. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo kaya toast reference and USDA SR Legacy component data for white bread, kaya jam, and butter. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation rather than direct laboratory analysis of this specific product.
notes: 1. Cuisine corrected from 'Italian' (input error) to 'Singaporean' — kaya toast is a Singaporean kopitiam staple with Hainanese origins; the Italian/Pizza/Coffee tags in the input reflect the restaurant's broader menu, not the dish's cuisine identity. 2. Food name normalised from menu-style 'T10) ALL TOAST BREAD WITH KAYA / BUTTER / PEANUT ,ETC 2 PCS' to canonical 'Kaya Toast with Butter (2 slices)'. 3. Serving size set to 80g (2 standard thin slices with spreads). 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as white bread, kaya jam, and butter contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy component data. 5. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite; no -1 sentinel values used as reasonable estimates are available from approved sources. 6. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for kaya toast composite exists in the literature. 7. Peanut butter variant noted in food name; peanut butter would alter fat profile (increasing MUFA/PUFA) and allergen profile; this record represents the standard kaya-butter version as the canonical form.
